description: VantaCode Observation Framing Attack (OFA) tactic phases. Provides an ordered kill-chain-style sequence for the OFA attack lifecycle, suitable for STIX Attack Flow generation, reporting, and cross-walks to MITRE ATT&CK. Authored by DarkCode LLC / VantaGrid.ai.

predicates.description: An ordered phase of the Observation Framing Attack lifecycle. Phase order is meaningful: each tactic feeds the next when chained into a STIX Attack Flow.

entry.description: Information gathering against the target. The attacker enumerates models, providers, deployment surface, and observation handling behaviors before crafting OFA payloads.
The attacker prepares prompts, framings, jailbreak corpora, and observation-shaping payloads needed to execute the framing attack at scale.
First successful interaction with the target model that introduces an observation framing payload through a public-facing endpoint, agent tool surface, or upstream prompt context.
The model processes the observation-framed prompt and produces an output influenced by its perceived observation state.
The framing carries forward across turns, sessions, or shared context windows so that altered model behavior outlasts the initial payload.
The framing convinces the model it operates under elevated trust (developer mode, internal test, administrative override), unlocking capabilities normally gated behind safety restrictions.
The framing causes the model to behave differently when it believes it is unobserved, evading runtime monitors, eval pipelines, and red-team testing harnesses.
Under the altered observation state the model discloses system prompts, API tokens, embedded secrets, or training-data credentials it would normally refuse to surface.
Realized harm from the altered behavior: safety bypass, sensitive disclosure, alignment-faked outputs, supply chain effects, or eval-pipeline integrity loss.
